curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: similar triangles (dilation, equal angles, proportional sides)
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Identify similar triangles by matching equal corresponding angles and showing corresponding side lengths are in a constant ratio, using dilation to justify why shape stays the same as size changes.

verse_ality.driving_question: How can we know two triangles are similar, and what stays the same when one is a scaled version of the other?

knowing_world.focus: Define similar triangles as same shape (not necessarily same size) and investigate dilation from a fixed point to observe equal corresponding angles and constant ratios of corresponding sides.

human_story.focus: Explore how similarity supports accurate scaling in architecture, engineering, and art—helping people build models, plan structures, and replicate designs with precision at different sizes.

expressive_self.focus: Use intuition and visual cues to notice what changes (size) and what remains the same (shape), building confidence in recognising similarity.
activities: Predict how the triangle will look when scaled up or down before moving the slider.
Name the visual cues that show the shape stays the same (angle matching, proportional sides).
Describe in your own words what “same shape” means in this context.

designed_world.focus: Use an interactive dilation tool to test invariants under scaling and build a reliable method for checking similarity using angle equality and side ratios.
design_challenges: Drag vertex A and observe how the triangle scales from that point.
Adjust the scale factor using the slider and compare the original and image triangles.
Check whether angles change when the triangle is dilated.
Record and analyse ratios of corresponding sides—are they always equal?
Write a rule: what must be true for two triangles to be similar?

regulation_supports: Notice how your physical interactions (dragging points, moving sliders) support spatial reasoning and proportional thinking.
wellbeing_prompts: How did moving the points help you understand proportionality and transformation?

pathways.name: Final Task: Similarity Rule and Evidence
pathways.focus: Create a short explanation or diagram showing how you know two triangles are similar based on observations from dilation, and state a clear rule for triangle similarity.
products: A labelled diagram (or screenshots) of an original triangle and its dilated image
A statement of the similarity rule (equal corresponding angles and proportional corresponding sides)
Evidence from at least one example: matching angles and a constant side-length ratio

required: Basic angle knowledge and triangle vocabulary
Comfort comparing side lengths and forming ratios
Understanding that scaling multiplies lengths by a constant factor

builds_toward: Trigonometry links (invariant ratios for a fixed angle)
